interpretation: Every independent trial should complete end to end.
Metrics must be finite and lie in expected bounds.
The learned ranker should usually improve hybrid retrieval.
Median lift guards against one lucky seed.
The lower tail should remain useful under stress scenarios.
Top-k should recover a useful fraction of the maximum relevant items that can fit in top-k.
Calibration error should remain controlled.
Small-data serving latency must stay within the configured local budget.
A similar-to query should not simply return its anchor item.
No approved-data Monte Carlo trial should violate a hard claim/privacy boundary.
Anchor and personalized-user groups should not cross data splits.
Synthetic trials should evaluate against latent clean relevance rather than noisy observed labels.
Paired long-tail trials should increase popularity inequality.
Paired sparse-metadata trials should reduce observed tag coverage.
Paired sparse-metadata trials should reduce visual-query quality.
Paired high-noise trials should increase rating dispersion.
Paired high-noise trials should weaken preference-to-rating correlation.
Paired high-noise trials should reduce median clean-truth NDCG.
A majority of paired high-noise trials should degrade ranking quality.
Paired high-noise trials should increase observed-vs-clean label disagreement.
